definition:
1. stated, specified , (Iliad by Homer); ἐς χρόνον ῥητόν at a set  or stated  time, (Herdotus Historicus); ἡμέραι ῥ. (Thucydides); ἐπὶ ῥητοῖς γέρασι with fixed  prerogatives, (Thucydides); ῥ. ἀργύριον a stated  sum, (Thucydides); ἐπὶ ῥητοῖς on stated terms, on certain conditions , (Herdotus Historicus):—;adverb ῥητῶς, expressly, distinctly , (New Testament) 
2. spoken of, known, famous , (Hesiod) 
3. that may be spoken  or told , (Aeschulus Tragicus); ῥητὸν ἄρρητόν τ᾽ ἔπος, Lat. fas nefasque, (Sophocles Tragicus) 
4. in Mathem., ῥητά are rational quantities , opposite to surds  ἄλογα); (Plato Philosophus) (ML)
